volumeTitle: The Little Carthaginian. Pseudolus. The Rope
label: The Little Carthaginian (Section 3.2.578b - Section 3.3.637)

sections.label: Section 3.2.578b
sections.translation: Nicely .

sections.label: Section 3.2.578c
sections.translation: Take care you understand them , please .

sections.label: Section 3.2.579
sections.original: Quid opust verbis ? callum aprugnum callere aeque non sinam .
sections.translation: What need is there of talking ? I won ’ t let my own legs understand [*] as well .

sections.label: Section 3.2.580
sections.original: Fac modo ut condocta tibi sint dicta ad hanc fallaciam .
sections.translation: Only take you care that your speeches are learnt by heart for this plot .

sections.label: Section 3.2.581
sections.original: Quin edepol condoctior sum , quam tragoedi aut comici .
sections.translation: Why , upon my faith , I am more perfect than tragic or comic actors are .

sections.label: Section 3.2.582
sections.translation: You are a capital fellow .

sections.label: Section 3.2.582b
sections.translation: Let ’ s go nearer to them .

sections.label: Section 3.2.582c
sections.translation: ( Accosting MILPHIO and COLLYBISCUS .) Here are the witnesses .

sections.label: Section 3.2.583
sections.original: Non potuisti adducere homines magis ad hanc rem idoneos .
sections.translation: Really you could not have brought as many men better suited for this purpose ; for not one of them is tongue-tied as a witness [*] ; they are genuine men of the law-courts ;

sections.label: Section 3.2.585
sections.original: ibi habitant , ibi eos conspicias quam praetorem saepius .
sections.translation: there they take up their abode ; there you may see them more frequently than the Praetor . At this very time there are no better cookers-up of a lawsuit [*] , to stir up litigation , than are these men ; for they , if there is no litigation , sow litigation .

sections.label: Section 3.2.588
sections.translation: May the Gods confound you !

sections.label: Section 3.2.588b
sections.original: Vos quidem hercle — cum eo cum quiqui tamen
sections.translation: You I really do commend , inasmuch as , whoever you are , still you act both worthily and kindly in giving your aid to my master thus in love .

sections.label: Section 3.2.590
sections.original: sed isti iam sciunt , negoti quid sit ?
sections.translation: ( To AGORASTOCLES .) But do they now know what the business is ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.590b
sections.translation: The whole matter , all in its order .

sections.label: Section 3.2.591
sections.original: Tum vos animum advortite igitur . hunc vos lenonem Lycum
sections.translation: In that case , do you , then , give me your attention . Do you know this Procurer Lycus ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.592b
sections.translation: Perfectly .

sections.label: Section 3.2.592c
sections.original: At pol ego eum , qua sit facie , nescio .
sections.translation: But , upon my faith , I don ’ t know him , of what appearance he is . I wish that you would point this fellow out to me .

sections.label: Section 3.2.593b
sections.translation: We ’ ll take all care : we ’ ve been instructed quite enough .

sections.label: Section 3.2.594b
sections.translation: He has got three hundred pieces counted out .

sections.label: Section 3.2.595
sections.original: Ergo nos inspicere oportet istuc aurum , Agorastocles ,
sections.translation: Then it ’ s right , Agorastocles , that we should see this gold , that we may know what to say by-and-by as our testimony .

sections.label: Section 3.2.597
sections.translation: Come and look at it .

sections.label: Section 3.2.597b
sections.original: Aurum est profecto hoc , spectatores , comicum :
sections.translation: Undoubtedly it ’ s gold , Spectators — playhouse gold [*] ; upon this , soaked in water , in foreign lands , the cattle become fat [*] : but , for the carrying out of this design , ’ tis real Philippean gold . [*]

sections.label: Section 3.2.599
sections.original: verum ad hanc rem agundam Philippum est : ita nos adsimulabimus .
sections.translation: We ’ ll make believe it is so .

sections.label: Section 3.2.600
sections.original: Sed ita adsimulatote quasi ego sim peregrinus .
sections.translation: But do you make believe as though I were a foreigner .

sections.label: Section 3.2.600b
sections.translation: Just so ; and , in fact , as though you , on your arrival to-day , had asked us to show you a spot for freedom and pleasure ; where you might wench , drink , and live like a Greek .

sections.label: Section 3.2.603b
sections.translation: Dear me ! Crafty fellows , upon my faith !

sections.label: Section 3.2.604
sections.translation: But it was I who instructed them .

sections.label: Section 3.2.604b
sections.translation: And who you , in your turn ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.604c
sections.translation: Come , be of ’ in-doors , Agorastocles ,

sections.label: Section 3.2.605
sections.original: ne hic vos mecum conspicetur leno neu fallaciae
sections.translation: lest the Procurer should see you together with me , and some accident might befall our plan .

sections.label: Section 3.2.606b
sections.translation: This person is extremely prudent . ( To AGORASTOCLES .) Do as he bids you .

sections.label: Section 3.2.607b
sections.translation: Let ’ s be off . ( To the ASSISTANTS .) But you —

sections.label: Section 3.2.607c
sections.translation: has enough been said ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.608
sections.translation: I ’ m off .

sections.label: Section 3.2.608b
sections.translation: Immortal Gods , I beg —

sections.label: Section 3.2.608c
sections.translation: I ’ m off .

sections.label: Section 3.2.608d
sections.translation: You do wisely . ( AGORASTOCLES and MILPHIO go into the house .)

sections.label: Section 3.2.608a
sections.translation: Hush ! be quiet .

sections.label: Section 3.2.609b
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.609c
sections.original: Fores haé fecerunt magnum flagitium modo .
sections.translation: This door ( pointing to the door of the house of LYCUS ) was guilty of a great indecency just now .

sections.label: Section 3.2.610
sections.translation: What indecency is that ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.610b
sections.translation: It rumbled aloud .

sections.label: Section 3.2.610c
sections.translation: May the Deities confound you ! Get you behind us .

sections.label: Section 3.2.611b
sections.translation: Be it so .

sections.label: Section 3.2.611c
sections.translation: We ’ ll walk first .

sections.label: Section 3.2.612
sections.original: Faciunt scurrae quod consuerunt : pone sese homines locant .
sections.translation: They do what town-fellows are in the habit of doing : they put worthy men behind themselves .

sections.label: Section 3.2.613
sections.original: Illic homo est , qui egreditur , leno .
sections.translation: That man that ’ s coming out is the Procurer .

sections.label: Section 3.2.613b
sections.translation: He ’ s a real good one ; for he ’ s like a bad man [*] . Even now , as he comes forth , I ’ ll suck out his blood at this distance .

sections.label: Section 3.3.615
sections.original: Iam ego istúc revortar , miles : convivas volo
sections.translation: I ’ ll return here this moment , Captain . I wish to find us some fitting guests , to join us . Meanwhile , they ’ ll bring the entrails ; and at the same time , the women , I suppose , will soon be making their appearance at home after the sacrifice . But why are such a number of people coming this way ? I wonder what they are bringing ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.620
sections.original: et illé chlamydatus quisnam est , qui sequitur procul ?
sections.translation: He , too , in the scarf , that ’ s following at a distance , who is he , I wonder ? He is not an Aetolian .

sections.label: Section 3.3.621
sections.translation: We greet you , Lycus . Although against our will , we give you this salutation , and although in a very moderate degree do we entertain good wishes for procurers .

sections.label: Section 3.3.623
sections.original: Fortunati omnes sitis , quod certo scio
sections.translation: May you all be fortunate — a thing that I know for certain you neither will be , nor will Fortune permit it so to be .

sections.label: Section 3.3.625
sections.original: Istic ést thensaurus stultis in lingua situs ,
sections.translation: That is a treasure hoarded in the tongues of fools , to deem it gainful to speak amiss to their superiors .

sections.label: Section 3.3.627
sections.original: Viam qui nescit , qua deveniat ad mare ,
sections.translation: He who knows not the road by which to arrive at the sea , him it befits to seek a river as his own companion . I know not the way of speaking abusively to you .

sections.label: Section 3.3.630
sections.original: nunc vos mihi amnes estis ; vos certum est sequi :
sections.translation: Now you are the rivers to me ; you I ’ m resolved to follow . If you speak blessings , along your banks I ’ ll follow you ; if you utter curses , along your track I ’ ll go .

sections.label: Section 3.3.633
sections.translation: To do good to the bad is a danger just as great as to do bad to the good .

sections.label: Section 3.3.634b
sections.translation: But why ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.634c
sections.translation: You shall learn .

sections.label: Section 3.3.635
sections.original: malo si quid bene facias , id beneficium interit ;
sections.translation: If you do any good to the bad , the benefit is lost : if you do any bad to the good , it lasts for a length of time .

sections.label: Section 3.3.637
sections.original: Facete dictum . sed quid istúc ad me attinet ?
sections.translation: Cleverly said ! But what does that matter to me ?
